name: Beef Vegetable Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the soup bones.
2. Place the bones on an aluminum-lined tray and brown them in a 400°F oven until well-browned on both sides.
3. Transfer the bones to a soup pot, removing any excess grease.
4. Collect as much of the browned bits using a little water.
5. Add enough water to cover the bones completely.
6. Turn on the heat.
7. Add carrots, onions, celery, tomatoes (as desired), salt, and pepper.
8. Allow the soup to simmer for most of the day.
9. Remove the bones and chop any remaining meat.
10. Discard the vegetables and skim off as much fat as possible (if left in the refrigerator overnight, it will solidify at the top and can be easily removed).
11. Return the chopped meat to the pot.
12. Add 3 quarts of whole tomatoes (mashed), a couple of cubed potatoes, and about half a package of frozen mixed vegetables.
13. Simmer until the potatoes are tender and the soup is ready.
14. The soup tastes better the next day.
15. Cook noodles if desired.
